page: Microsoft Azure D8pds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine belonging to the DPDSv5 server family. It is powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz, featuring 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs with no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is supported by a 307 GB disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks place the D8pds_v5 in the average tier for most general-purpose operations. It demonstrates solid multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 14512.61 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 5955.0. Memory bandwidth is robust for cached reads at 565.91 GB/sec, though it drops to 68.42 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. The server exhibits weaker performance in single-core tasks, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1055.0 and a poor single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 7.34 GB/sec.

Utilizing an ARM-based architecture allows this instance to offer a cost-efficient profile for workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading overhead. The inclusion of 307 GB of local storage and average database performance, such as 10.92 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, makes it suitable for web serving, software compilation, and database operations. However, it is less optimal for single-threaded compute-heavy tasks or intensive cryptographic operations due to its weaker single-core and encryption benchmark results.
description: Microsoft Azure D8pds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine from the DPDSv5 family, powered by an 8-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 307 GB local disk with Premium Storage capability. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14512.61 and a Redis SET throughput of 10.92 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1055.0 and lower single-threaded decompression speeds. This server offers a qualitatively cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded applications, making it well-suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that leverage its dedicated physical cores and local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 32 GB of RAM, and a 307 GB local disk, optimized for general-purpose and database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 8 dedicated cores, 32 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, suitable for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM-based virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores, balanced memory, and local storage for efficient multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor running at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 307 GB local storage disk
Features dedicated physical cores with no hyperthreading overhead
Delivers average multi-core performance and 10.92 million Redis SET operations per second
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database workloads
